blocks.narrative: Confirmed block types include lesson slides (card-based), MCQ quizzes, flip cards, key takeaways, examples, and actionable steps. Images and video can be added and edited per lesson. AI narration is generated automatically. However, the block palette does not appear to include advanced interactive types — accordion, tabs, hotspot image, timeline, table, code block, and drag-drop interactions are not confirmed in any public source. The AI-first generation model means authors work with a curated set of pedagogically-structured block types rather than a freeform block library. This is market standard for AI-native tools but below competitive depth for manual authoring. Evidence: [marketing|review]

assessment.narrative: MCQ quizzes are confirmed and AI-generated automatically from course content, with unlimited quiz generation mentioned as a feature. Knowledge checks and unscored review elements (key takeaways, flip cards) are confirmed. However, specific assessment question types beyond MCQ — true/false, fill-in-the-blank, matching, drag-drop, hotspot image, and essay/open-text — are not confirmed in public sources. Question banks, randomisation, passing score configuration, and multiple attempts are not confirmed. Assessment is functional but appears basic relative to dedicated assessment platforms. Evidence: [marketing|review]

ai.narrative: AI is the core differentiator and is deeply integrated throughout: AI course generation from PDFs, videos, PPTx, web links, and voice notes; AI quiz generation automatically per lesson; AI content rewrite and editing assistance; AI narration (lifelike audio) for all courses; and AI translation into 120+ languages. AI image generation and AI learner feedback/tutoring are not confirmed. The AI-first workflow — where the entire course structure is generated before manual editing — places Courseau among the most AI-native tools in the market. Evidence: [marketing|review]

compliance.narrative: SCORM export is confirmed and available for LMS delivery. Editable PowerPoint export is also confirmed as an output format. xAPI/Tin Can is not confirmed in any public source for Courseau's standalone product. LTI (consumer or provider) is not confirmed. PDF export is not confirmed. The compliance story is functional for organisations needing basic SCORM LMS delivery, but lacks the breadth of SCORM 2004, xAPI, and LTI that enterprise authoring tools offer. Evidence: [marketing|review]

analytics.narrative: Courseau is described in marketing as an authoring tool rather than a learning platform, and no analytics dashboard is confirmed in the standalone product. The extensibility screenshot shows a feature comparison table but analytics rows are not clearly visible. Completion tracking and quiz score analytics may be available when courses are delivered via SCORM to an LMS, but those would be LMS-side features, not Courseau's own. Learner-side analytics within Courseau's shared-link delivery mode are not confirmed. LearnUpon integration (planned 2026) would add LMS analytics. Not confirmed from available access. Evidence: [marketing]

collaboration.narrative: Real-time commenting is confirmed — G2 reviewers and marketing describe collaborating 'with your team using real-time comments for instant feedback.' Multi-author access is implied by the team collaboration feature. However, structured review/approval workflows, version history, external reviewer links (without account), and granular roles and permissions are not confirmed in public sources. Collaboration features appear functional but lightweight compared to tools like Elucidat with full review workflow and audit trails. Evidence: [marketing|review]

templates.narrative: The AI-first generation model means Courseau does not rely on traditional course templates — the AI produces a structured draft from source material. Some pre-built lesson structures (key takeaways, flip cards, examples) function as implicit templates. Theme customisation (branding, colours) is mentioned in marketing. White-label and custom domain features are not confirmed. A traditional library of named course or lesson templates does not appear to be part of the product model. Evidence: [marketing|review]

branching.narrative: Conditional navigation and personalised learning paths are not confirmed in any public source for Courseau's standalone product. The platform's slide/card sequential structure does not suggest a branching-native architecture. LearnUpon's broader LMS platform supports Learning Journeys (automated personalised paths), but this is an LMS feature, not Courseau's authoring output. Branching is effectively absent from the confirmed Courseau feature set. Not confirmed from available access. Evidence: [marketing|review]

media.narrative: Direct video upload and image upload/editing are confirmed. AI narration (audio generation) is a strong, native media feature. Images and videos can be added and edited within the editor. A stock media library is not confirmed. In-product image editing depth is unclear beyond basic insertion. Subtitle/caption support for uploaded video is not confirmed. CDN hosting is implied by the link-based delivery model. Evidence: [marketing|review]

accessibility.narrative: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance is not confirmed in any public source for Courseau. Keyboard navigation and screen reader support are not mentioned in marketing or reviews. The platform does confirm translation into 120+ languages and AI narration as accessibility-adjacent features (multilingual, audio support), but these are not substitutes for WCAG technical compliance. Given the platform's AI-native, modern build, baseline accessibility is probable but not confirmed from available access. Evidence: [marketing]

publishing.narrative: Six publishing modes are confirmed: shareable link, QR code, SCORM export, website embed, editable PowerPoint, and live embed. Password protection (master password for access restriction) is confirmed in help docs. SSO for learner access is not confirmed. SCORM export enables LMS delivery. xAPI is not confirmed. The publishing feature set covers the most common mid-market needs but does not reach enterprise depth (SCORM 2004, xAPI, instant re-release, SSO). Evidence: [marketing|review]

extensibility.narrative: No REST API, webhooks, Zapier, or Make integrations are confirmed for the Courseau standalone product. The pricing/features table visible in the extensibility screenshot does not surface API or integration rows. SCORM export and link sharing are the primary integration mechanisms. LearnUpon's acquisition (November 2025) signals deeper integration with LearnUpon LMS in 2026, but as of the current product state, extensibility is minimal. No MCP server confirmed. Evidence: [marketing]

rich_text.notes: Lesson text content is confirmed as core block type

image.notes: Images can be added and edited within lessons

video_embed.notes: Video can be added to lessons; embed method not specified

video_upload.notes: Direct video upload confirmed as source input for AI generation

audio.notes: AI narration generates audio per lesson; manual audio upload not confirmed

code_block.notes: Not a target use case for this platform

flashcard.notes: Flip cards confirmed as a native block/element type

mcq.notes: MCQ quizzes confirmed; AI-generated automatically from course content

true_false.notes: Not explicitly confirmed; may be included in quiz generation

hotspot_image.notes: Not confirmed; unlikely given current block palette

knowledge_check_unscored.notes: Key takeaways and flip cards serve as unscored knowledge reinforcement

ai_course_generation.notes: Core feature — generates full course structure from PDFs, video, PPTx, web links, voice notes

ai_content_rewrite.notes: AI editing and content refinement supported post-generation

ai_quiz_generation.notes: Unlimited AI quiz generation from course content confirmed

scorm_12.notes: SCORM export confirmed; version not explicitly specified in public sources

pdf_export.notes: Not confirmed; PowerPoint export is confirmed instead

completion_tracking.notes: Not confirmed in standalone Courseau; available via SCORM delivery to LMS

quiz_score_analytics.notes: Not confirmed in standalone Courseau product

analytics_dashboard.notes: Not confirmed from available access; LearnUpon LMS provides analytics

multi_author.notes: Team collaboration and multi-author editing confirmed

inline_commenting.notes: Real-time comments on course content confirmed in marketing and G2 reviews

course_templates.notes: AI generates structured drafts; no traditional named template library confirmed

lesson_templates.notes: Implicit lesson structures (key takeaways, flip cards, quizzes) act as template patterns

conditional_navigation.notes: Not confirmed; platform is sequential slide/card-based

personalised_paths.notes: Not confirmed in standalone Courseau; LearnUpon Learning Journeys is a separate LMS feature

direct_video_upload.notes: Video upload confirmed as source input and in-lesson media

cdn_hosting.notes: Implied by link-based shareable delivery model

in_product_image_editing.notes: Images can be added and edited; depth of editing tools not confirmed

shareable_link_no_lms.notes: Direct link sharing confirmed as primary delivery method

password_protection.notes: Master password for access restriction confirmed in help docs

embed_in_external_site.notes: Website embed and live embed both confirmed as sharing modes

rest_api.notes: Not confirmed from available access; no public API documentation found

sso_author_access.notes: Not confirmed from available access; possible via LearnUpon post-acquisition
